question_text: Match the following terms to the correct example:

answers.text: The difference in electric potential between two points in a circuit, measured in volts (V). It represents the work done to move a unit charge from one point to another.
The flow of electric charge through a conductor, measured in amperes (A). It is the rate at which charge passes through a point in a circuit.
A device used to measure the voltage or potential difference between two points in a circuit.
The terminal of a battery or cell where current flows in.
A component that limits or regulates the flow of electric current in a circuit.
The terminal of a battery or cell where current flows out.
A device that produces light when an electric current passes through it.
A material that does not allow electric current to flow through it.
An electric current that flows in one direction only.
A graphical representation of an electrical circuit using symbols for components.
The individual parts or elements that make up an electrical circuit.
A single unit that generates electrical energy through chemical reactions.
A machine that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy.
A material that allows electric current to flow through it easily.
An electric current that periodically reverses direction.
An audio signalling device that produces a buzzing sound when an electric current passes through it.
A device used to open or close a circuit, controlling the flow of electricity.
An instrument used to measure electric current in a circuit.
